prompt:
Use case: logo-brand
Asset type: fourth-round product app icon exploration for CopayGuard
Primary request: same defensive pill mascot holding an oversized shield, but translated into a dark developer-tool icon vibe with orange technical/pixel linework
Subject: a simple pill-shaped mascot defending itself with a shield larger than its body; pill is mostly hidden behind the large shield; one visible left hand grips the shield edge; shield pushed forward as a protective block
Style/medium: minimalist app icon, dark rounded-square background, geometric technical illustration, pixel-grid and terminal-inspired linework, subtle 8-bit/block construction, clean vector-friendly shapes, sparse details, premium developer-tool aesthetic
Composition/framing: centered inside a dark rounded square, shield dominates the mark, pill peeks from behind it, large readable silhouette, no scene, app-icon-ready at small size
Color palette: near-black charcoal background, graphite gray shapes, warm orange/copper linework, muted amber highlights, off-white tiny highlights only; gray/orange brand palette
Text (verbatim): none
Constraints: no written text, no letters, no wordmark, no watermark, no photorealism, no red cross, no Claude logo, no Anthropic starburst, no asterisk/sunburst, no exact imitation of any existing brand mark
Avoid: teal, blue, green, purple, red, busy background, cute toy look, detailed face, medical-cross symbol, copied Claude Code icon, fake text
Variation: shield built from chunky orange pixel-like strokes and charcoal fills, pill silhouette tucked behind the left edge, simple two-dot face barely visible.
